_about: Test series — lighthouse keeper Walter, Petit Manan Light, coastal Maine.
style_anchor: Coastal Maine documentary in the Tartakovsky cel-animation tradition. Hand-painted backgrounds, thick black ink outlines, flat color fills with NO gradients. Cool dawn key light from the east, warm interior lamp light from the lighthouse window, salt-fog haze in the midground. Consistent character design across every frame.
world_sheet: Setting: Petit Manan Light on a granite islet, six miles off the rocky coast of Maine, late autumn 1962. Time: pre-dawn, just before sunrise. Atmosphere: thick salt fog rolling in from the open Atlantic, the foghorn pulsing every 30 seconds in the distance, gulls wheeling overhead, weathered cedar shingles slick with cold mist. Mood: solitary, mythic, weight of unbroken routine.

character_sheet.walter: Man in his early 60s, weathered tanned face with deep crow's-feet, full salt-and-pepper beard trimmed close, watery blue eyes, dark navy wool watch cap pulled low, oiled canvas coat in faded mustard yellow with brass buttons, thick leather gloves clutching a battered tin coffee mug with steam rising. Body language: deliberate, steady, never rushed. Always shown in 3/4 profile facing the sea unless specified.
character_sheet.lighthouse: White-painted granite tower, 119 feet tall, narrow vertical fresnel window at the top with warm amber light visible through the fog, black iron lantern-room cap, attached one-story keeper's quarters in weathered cedar shingles with green-painted shutters.
